Experience building models and conducting statistical or quantitative research.

The majority of our employees can live and work anywhere in the U.S or Canada (outside of Quebec) but are expected to spend high quality time in-person collaborating via regular onsites and in-person meetings. jobs.title: AI Scout
jobs.description_full:
About the job

AI Scout - Commission-Based Partnership




Kerzie AI Solutions | Remote | Flexible | 1099 Contractor




HOW TO APPLY




Visit: https://kerzieconsulting.com/ai-scout-opportunity




Click the "Apply Now" button and fill out the application form to apply.




ABOUT THE ROLE:




Kerzie AI Solutions is building a network of AI Scouts - driven individuals who identify Small businesses struggling with customer communication and connect them with Unison, our AI-powered solution that handles phone calls, texts, website chat/voice, and social media channels from a single knowledge base.




This is not a traditional sales job. You are a scout - someone who spots opportunity before others do. You find the HVAC company whose calls go to voicemail after 5pm. The dental office drowning in appointment requests. The property manager who cannot keep up with tenant inquiries. You get the contract signed; we handle the rest.




How you find businesses is up to you. Tap your existing network, join a business networking group, cold call, knock on doors, run ads, leverage social media, attend local events - whatever works. We care about results, not methods.




Success in this role looks like: Building a portfolio of 15+ clients generating $2,900+/month in recurring commission, while helping small businesses deliver consistent customer experiences around the clock. There is no ceiling on this. You can do the math to see what your exit from the 9-5 treadmill looks like if that is your goal. This can also be a lucrative side hustle. This is different for every scout. The opportunity is there if you want it. 




How this fits into Kerzie AI: Scouts are our frontline growth engine. While our team focuses on building and delivering Unison, you are out in the world finding the businesses who need it most. As we scale toward 300 clients, scouts who get in early will build substantial recurring income streams.




COMMISSION STRUCTURE




Here is where this opportunity is different from anything you will find in corporate America.




Most sales roles pay you once and move on. Maybe you get a small residual for a quarter or two. Then it is back to zero, chasing the next deal just to stay employed.




This is a monthly recurring commission structure - and it is virtually unheard of at these rates:




1-2 contracts: 20%

3-9 contracts: 35%

10+ contracts: 50%




Example earnings at $500/month per client:

- 5 clients = $725/month recurring

- 10 clients = $1,675/month recurring

- 20 clients = $4,175/month recurring




IMPORTANT: THIS IS A 1099 CONTRACTOR POSITION




This is not a W-2 employee role. You are an independent contractor. There is no base salary, no hourly wage, and no guaranteed income. Your earnings are 100% commission-based, determined entirely by the contracts you bring in.




That said, the upside is real: every contract you sign generates monthly recurring income for as long as that customer continues paying for Unison. You are not building someone else's book of business - you are building your own.




RESPONSIBILITIES




- Get Unison contracts signed




- Identify small and mid-sized businesses that struggle with customer communication (missed calls, slow response times, lost leads, inconsistent messaging across channels)




- Acquire leads through any method you choose - networking groups, cold outreach, social media, local events, referrals, or creative approaches we have not thought of




- Understand businesses current communication challenges




- Qualify leads by assessing fit for Unison




- Introduce qualified prospects to the Kerzie AI team for onboarding




- Maintain relationships with referred clients to identify additional opportunities and gather feedback




- Track your pipeline and referrals using provided tools and systems




WHAT WE PROVIDE




You do not need to be an AI expert. You do not need to know how to build automations. We will train you on everything you need to know - how to talk to business owners about Unison, how to explain automation in simple terms, and how to identify the pain points that make a business a great fit. You show up ready to learn and hustle; we will make sure you are prepared to get contracts signed.




QUALIFICATIONS




Required:




- Strong interpersonal and communication skills - you are comfortable starting conversations with business owners




-Experienced in B2B sales or consulting




- Self-motivated with the ability to work independently without daily supervision




- Coachable - willing to learn how we talk about AI and automation so you can confidently represent Unison




- Resourcefulness in finding and reaching potential clients through whatever channels work for you




- Reliable internet access and smartphone




Preferred:




- Familiarity with local business communities (Existing network, Chamber of Commerce, BNI, industry associations, etc.)




- Understanding of how small businesses operate - their challenges, constraints, and priorities




Not Required:




- Technical background or coding experience




- AI expertise or experience building automations - we teach you everything you need to know




- Full-time availability - You choose how much time you would like to invest in this opportunity.




ABOUT KERZIE AI SOLUTIONS




Kerzie AI Solutions helps small businesses deliver consistent, high-quality customer communication across every channel - 24/7. Our flagship product, Unison, combines telecom expertise with applied AI to handle phone calls, website chat/voice, SMS, and social media channel inquiries from a unified knowledge base.




We believe every business deserves the customer communication infrastructure of a Fortune 500 company. Unison makes that possible at $500/month with no hidden fees or per-message charges.




Learn more about Kerzie AI and Unison: https://kerzie.ai




HOW TO APPLY




Visit: https://kerzieconsulting.com/ai-scout-opportunity




Click the "Apply Now" button and fill out the application form to apply.

jobs.title: AI Community Moderator (EST)
jobs.description_full:
About the job

Community Moderator - AI Advantage

Location: Remote in the United States (EST preferred)

Schedule: Full-time in EST (40 hours/week) + occasional weekend coverage for live events

Salary: $45,000-$50,000




To Apply: Please review the entire job description and follow the application instructions under the HOW TO APPLY section. 




ABOUT THE AI ADVANTAGE




Founded by Tony Robbins, Dean Graziosi, and Igor Pogany, AI Advantage exists to help everyday entrepreneurs, professionals, and business owners use AI confidently to reclaim their time and amplify their results. We're building the best AI education community in the world for beginners and intermediates—cutting through the confusion and making AI simple, practical, and powerful for regular people.




Our mission: Help people create real leverage in their personal and professional lives so they can get time and energy back to do the things they love.







THE ROLE




As a Community Moderator, you are the heartbeat of the AI Advantage experience. You'll be the first point of contact for our members, providing lightning-fast support, fostering genuine connections, and ensuring every member feels valued and empowered.




This role requires a very special kind of person: You're a natural people-person who genuinely loves serving others and pouring value into their lives. You find deep satisfaction in helping members succeed, answering their questions with warmth, and being a steady, reliable presence they can count on. While AI education is part of the work, you thrive in a role where consistency matters—where showing up with the same exceptional care and attention every single day is what creates extraordinary member experiences.




When you're on shift, you're 100% dedicated to our members—this is your sole focus.







WHAT YOU'LL DO




Be the Front Line of Member Experience

Respond to member questions and comments within minutes across all community channels
Moderate live events, training sessions, and ongoing discussions with warmth and professionalism
Proactively engage members to foster connection and maintain a welcoming environment




Build Relationships That Matter

Handle conversations across multiple community spaces (forums, live events, DMs, channels)
Conduct thoughtful 1:1 DM conversations to understand member needs and goals
De-escalate concerns with empathy and professionalism
Identify natural opportunities to guide members toward higher-tier offerings when appropriate




Master the Content

Stay current on all community resources: guides, hacks of the week, live trainings, and AI tools
Direct members to the right resources quickly and accurately
Translate AI concepts into accessible, practical guidance for beginners and intermediates




Maintain Excellence

Response Time: Reply within minutes during your shift
Response Quality: Deliver accurate, helpful, empathetic communication
Conversation Quality: Create meaningful interactions that build trust and loyalty




WHAT YOU BRING




Required:

2+ years of experience in customer-facing roles (customer success, community management, customer support, or similar)
2+ years actively using AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and other LLMs at a beginner-to-intermediate level (you don't need to be an expert, but you should be familiar with these tools and use them regularly)
Exceptional written communication: Clear, warm, and professional—with impeccable grammar
High EQ: You read between the lines, sense what people need, and adapt your approach naturally
Genuine love for serving people: You get energy from helping others succeed and find fulfillment in being a consistent, supportive presence
Ultra-responsive: Thrive in a fast-paced environment where quick, quality responses matter
Highly organized: Manage multiple conversations simultaneously without dropping the ball
Self-directed: Work independently and take ownership of your shift
Comfortable with consistency: You excel at delivering the same high standard of care day after day, finding meaning in the reliability and impact of your work




Preferred:

1+ years of experience in online education, e-learning, or EdTech environments (big plus!)
Previous experience in paid communities or subscription products
Coaching, training, or facilitation experience




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS




Salary Range: $40,000–$55,000 annually (based on experience and location)

Benefits: Health Insurance, PTO, 401k match. 




TIME ZONE REQUIREMENTS




We're hiring 1 moderator to provide the following coverage:




Eastern Time (EST/EDT)




You must be located in and available during core business hours for your respective time zone. Occasional weekend availability required for live events (typically 1-2x monthly).




WHY THIS ROLE




If you’re someone who is AI-fluent, genuinely excited about helping others learn and apply AI in practical ways, and energized by being on the front lines of an active, fast-moving online community - this role was built for you.




We’re looking for people who:




Naturally show up with warmth, clarity, and responsiveness
Enjoy translating complex tools into simple, usable guidance
Take pride in being a consistent, reliable presence for members
Stay curious and current with AI trends because you want to, not because you’re told to
Believe great community moderation is about serving first, building trust, and creating real momentum for people




If you want to grow with a brand at the intersection of AI, education, and real human impact, we’d love to hear from you.




📬 HOW TO APPLY




Email the following to andres@mastermind.com with the subject line:

“AI Community Moderator – [Your Name]”




Please include in your email: 

1️⃣ Resume

Highlight relevant experience in community moderation, customer support, education, or customer-facing roles (AI experience is a big plus).




2️⃣ Written Response (1–2 pages max)




Please respond thoughtfully to all three prompts below:




AI Fluency & Learning Habits - Which AI tools do you actively use today (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude, NotebookLM)? How do you stay current on AI updates, trends, or new use cases?
Community Moderation Experience - Describe your experience supporting or moderating online communities. Which platforms have you worked in (e.g., Circle, Skool, Slack, Discord), and what did great moderation look like in practice?

How would you respond in a way that delivers value first — and, if appropriate, naturally guides them toward a higher-level program without being salesy?




Final Note

We review applications carefully. Clear, thoughtful responses matter more than polished buzzwords. If this role resonates with how you naturally show up, we encourage you to apply.

jobs.title: Handshake AI Fellow Experience Specialist, Contract
jobs.description_full:
About the job

About Handshake

Handshake is the career network for the AI economy. 20 million knowledge workers, 1,600 educational institutions, 1 million employers (including 100% of the Fortune 50), and every foundational AI lab trust Handshake to power career discovery, hiring, and upskilling, from freelance AI training gigs to first internships to full-time careers and beyond. This unique value is leading to unparalleled growth; in 2025, we tripled our ARR at scale.

Why Join Handshake Now


Shape how every career evolves in the AI economy, at global scale, with impact your friends, family and peers can see and feel
Work hand-in-hand with world-class AI labs, Fortune 500 partners and the world’s top educational institutions
Join a team with leadership from Scale AI, Meta, xAI, Notion, Coinbase, and Palantir, among others
Build a massive, fast-growing business with billions in revenue


About The Role

Handshake is expanding into AI research and development, building a world-class network of expert Fellows - PhDs, researchers, and subject matter specialists - who contribute to cutting-edge AI through specialized research, model evaluation, and domain-specific applications. Handshake is looking to bring on a Handshake AI Support Specialist, Contractor to provide payments, contracts, and general support to our Handshake MOVE Fellows. Reporting to the Senior Manager of Handshake AI Support, this role is a 6 month contract with the possibility of contract extension or conversion to a permanent role upon evaluation of fit for the role and company needs. If you are resourceful and attuned to detail, love digging into a complex and technical product, building strong relationships with customers and team members, and solving a wide variety of questions, bugs, and workflow needs - this is the role for you!

This is not a typical support job where you’ll exclusively work on tickets; it’s an opportunity to make Handshake AI feel like the premier destination for experts looking to challenge the boundaries of AI. You’ll have opportunities to interact with both Handshake leadership and our expert network directly.

You will work closely with our Handshake AI Ops, Finance, Product, Engineering, Projects and Fellow Experience (FEXP) teams to provide efficient and effective support, and contribute to an overall seamless experience for Fellows. You will primarily focus on handling payment questions, disputes, and managing contracts to ensure our Fellows have a smooth experience. You will also assist with general Handshake AI support, help unblock our users, share trends and learnings with the Handshake AI Team, and continue to improve the customer experience and our team operations. As an advocate for our Fellows, you’ll improve the Handshake AI experience and make an impact on our mission to build the infrastructure for the next era of human-AI collaboration.

Exceptional team members may have the ability to grow their career into new roles across the Handshake AI Ops team.


Contribute 40 hours per week during our business hours, Monday through Friday, and occasional overtime as needed. Provide amazing email, zoom, and/or phone support to Handshake MOVE Fellows via email, internal ticketing systems, and other channels. Your day-to-day will consist of:
80%: resolving questions and disputes, removing or updating contracts reporting bugs, sharing feedback, and other general inquiries. You’ll typically handle up to 75-100 tickets per day.
10% on priority initiatives or improving business process
5%: attending team meetings (1-3 per week)
5%: completing administrative tasks (emails, checking daily announcements, training, etc.)
Tickets will involve highly technical troubleshooting and workflows, building and troubleshooting custom reports using Big Query, SQL, Google Sheets, and/or Looker, and making sure to provide tailored, personal responses while building relationships
Use app environments, troubleshooting tools, internal and external documentation, and team communication channels, to find the quality answer for a wide range of users
Maintain a standard level of excellence in regards to response times, resolution times, equal share of queue, and the quality of the customer support experience
Be both a support specialist and an experienced consultant for Fellows
Utilize internal tools to manage issues between Operations, Finance, Product, Engineering, Project, and Support teams
Analyze common issues that Fellows have and coordinate with our Product and Ops teams to ideate and implement solutions

Desired Capabilities


1 - 3 years of support, Sales Development Representative or similar experience
BI tools: Experience with or strong aptitude to quickly learn Big Query, SQL, Google Sheets advanced formulas and analysis, and Looker to pull payment and project data and compare with our tracking systems
Self-Starter: Excited to approach new challenges and have a high bar for personal excellence
Technical Aptitude: Ability to learn technical tools and concepts quickly
Resilience: Comfort with change and ambiguity. We’re a growing startup and always refining processes, tools, etc!
Teamwork: Connects with teammates and actively builds a sense of community on the Handshake AI and Support teams. Collaborates with manager and colleagues and team on daily or weekly guidance on ticket workflows and prioritization.
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills with a clear ability to communicate complex topics with simple language.
Results oriented and ownership: Ability to maintain or exceed ticket and quality goals. High level of self accountability and integrity for processes and metrics.
Passion for the problem: Desire to help give people the chance to build the career they want, no matter where they're from or what school they attend
EQ: A strong sense of empathy with users of our products and cross functional partners
Critical Thinking: High attention to detail and ability to troubleshoot with limited information and a clear plan of action.
Expertise and Curiosity: Retains knowledge, shows ability to partially solve issues independently, and asks questions to increase personal knowledge. Isn’t afraid to do independent research on third party tools and systems to find answers.
Growth Mindset: Positive, eager to learn, utilizes resources, takes and gives feedback, connects with support team and key cross team stakeholders.
Diversity and Inclusion: Eagerness to contribute to and help build a diverse and inclusive team and exhibit these values internally and externally with customers or users.
Coverage: Can provide a regular schedule to provide consistent assistance to users. Based in the U.S. and is not a current student.


Extra Credit


Big Query, SQL, Google Sheets/Excel, Deel, and/or Hubstaff experience
ZenDesk experience
AI/LLM usage and training Experience
Programming, IT, data analysis or computer science courses, major or certifications
FinTech customer service experience
Previous experience working in an Enterprise SaaS Customer Support team at a fast-paced startup or hypergrowth tech company, or customer focused position involving technical knowledge of a company's products and services
Relevant experience in cross - functional communication or close partnerships with various teams (Success, Sales, Product etc)


Location & Work Expectations


No visa sponsorship available

jobs.title: Associate, Trust and Safety
jobs.description_full:
About the job

Our Mission

Healthcare should work for patients, but it doesn’t. In their time of need, they call down outdated insurance directories. Then wait on hold. Then wait weeks for the privilege of a visit. Then wait in a room solely designed for waiting. Then wait for a surprise bill. In any other consumer industry, the companies delivering such a poor customer experience would not survive. But in healthcare, patients lack market power. Which means they are expected to accept the unacceptable.

Zocdoc’s mission is to give power to the patient. To do that, we’ve built the leading healthcare marketplace that makes it easy to find and book in-person or virtual care in all 50 states, across +200 specialties and +12k insurance plans. By giving patients the ability to see and choose, we give them power. In doing so, we can make healthcare work like every other consumer sector, where businesses compete for customers, not the other way around. In time, this will drive quality up and prices down.

We’re 18 years old and the leader in our space, but we are still just getting started. If you like solving important, complex problems alongside deeply thoughtful, driven, and collaborative teammates, read on.

Your Impact on our Mission

Zocdoc’s most important asset is our people. As a Trust & Safety Associate, your mission is to promote patient and provider trust in the platform and protect the integrity of the marketplace by leading your team in detecting, reporting, and acting on abusive user behavior. As a member of this newly formalized function, you’ll be tasked with executing on Trust & Safety tasks as well as helping build additional structured Trust & Safety workflows in order to keep pace with Zocdoc’s rapidly growing and evolving marketplace.

Zocdoc’s Trust & Safety Operations function is at the nexus of our Community Standards, partnering with cross-functional teams such as Product, Data, Sales, Service, and Legal to execute on mitigating behavior and content that is out of line with the platform’s standards. As a Trust & Safety Associate, you’ll be responsible for the critical daily operations that determine how well Zocdoc’s marketplace represents its standards.

You’ll enjoy this role if you are…


Are a proactive operator who can bridge the gap between strategic policy and hands-on execution
Are a curious problem-solver who enjoys diving into data to uncover trends and patterns
Thrive in a fast-paced environment where you can identify issues and quickly communicate insights to the right stakeholders
Are personally motivated by seeing your work make a tangible impact on the safety of our users and the integrity of our platform, even if your contributions aren’t in the spotlight
Are comfortable navigating varying levels of ambiguity and can stay focused on the task at hand
Are confident in engaging in difficult conversations and decisions, especially in writing
Are proud of the quality of your work, but don't take yourself too seriously


Your day to day is…


Applying Zocdoc’s Community Standards to ensure our platform is a safe and respectful environment for all users
Investigating complex cases and user concerns, leveraging data and analytical skills to reach well-reasoned decisions. This includes managing high-volume email correspondence and oral communication with both internal and external stakeholders
Proactively identifying trends and emerging threats that could put Zocdoc or its users at risk, and escalating key insights to T&S leadership with urgency

jobs.title: Drone Solutions Specialist
jobs.description_full:
About the job

Who is Flock?

Flock Safety is the leading safety technology platform, helping communities thrive by taking a proactive approach to crime prevention and security. Our hardware and software suite connects cities, law enforcement, businesses, schools, and neighborhoods in a nationwide public-private safety network. Trusted by over 5,000 communities, 4,500 law enforcement agencies, and 1,000 businesses, Flock delivers real-time intelligence while prioritizing privacy and responsible innovation.

We’re a high-performance, low-ego team driven by urgency, collaboration, and bold thinking. Working at Flock means tackling big challenges, moving fast, and continuously improving. It’s intense but deeply rewarding for those who want to make an impact.

With nearly $700M in venture funding and a $7.5B valuation, we’re scaling intentionally and seeking top talent to help build the impossible. If you value teamwork, ownership, and solving tough problems, Flock could be the place for you.

The Opportunity

Are you a self-motivated, drone expert and established sales professional who wants to make money selling an innovative technology while also solving crime? If you thrive in a competitive, fast-paced, and mission-driven environment, this is a game-changing opportunity for you. Flock is looking to add a Drone Solutions Specialist to our growing aviation team. This person will play a key role in helping the company grow, focusing on a growth market, owning demonstrations, partnering with the GTM team, and supporting closing deals. The position requires travel 60% during peak seasons.

This is an individual contributor role that reports directly to our Director of Solution Specialists. This position will be quota carrying and has the opportunity to make commission on a quarterly basis tied to that individual quota for DFR.

This is a 100% remote field sales opportunity.

How You'll Make An Impact


Sales & Forecasting: Work closely with existing sales teams to work opportunities from start to finish ensuring you help the customer make the product decision. Continuously keeping a pulse on the pipeline, maintaining accurate records of sales activities, and providing forecasts to management.
New Business Development: Identify and pursue net new DFR opportunities for new business with prospective clients, understanding their requirements and proposing appropriate technology solutions.
Conduct Virtual and In-Person DFR Demonstrations: Support the existing sales team by being a DFR SME and adding value to their sales process. Cross functional collaboration is a must to help negotiate terms and ensure a successful deployment.
Market Research and Analysis: Stay updated on industry trends, competitors, and market developments, providing insights to inform sales strategies and product development.
Cross-functional Collaboration: Work closely with other teams such as sales, marketing, product development, and customer support to ensure alignment in messaging, product offerings, and customer satisfaction.
Continuous Learning and Skill Development: Stay updated on advancements in technology and sales techniques, continuously improving knowledge and skills to remain competitive in the industry.
Education of Flock’s entire product suite, understanding how all of our products work together cohesively in the ecosystem, knowing our goal is to enable customers to solve crimes faster and more efficiently, and be able to relay that to customers.
Attend Events: Represent Flock at DFR events, industry conferences, internal events with customers.


The Skillset


2-4 years of technical drone industry experience
4-6 years of sales experience, field sales preferred
Self-starter mentality with a willingness to learn, take initiative, and produce results
Technical product or software experience (SaaS) is a plus
Strong presentation and demonstration skills
Energized by solving customer's problems- e.g. consultative sales approach
Enjoy working in a fast paced, data-driven sales environment
Startup experience a plus


Feeling uneasy that you haven’t ticked every box? That’s okay, we’ve felt that way too. Studies have shown women and minorities are less likely to apply unless they meet all qualifications. We encourage you to break the status quo and apply to roles that would make you excited to come to work every day.

90 Days at Flock

We are a results-oriented culture and believe job descriptions are a thing of the past. We prescribe to 90 day plans and believe that good days, lead to good weeks, which lead to good months. This serves as a preview of the 90 day plan you will receive if you were to be hired in this role at Flock Safety.

The First 30 Days


Feel comfortable demo’ing Flock Aerodome platform and supporting sales team in customer meetings/calls
Thorough review of the DFR strategy
Understand internal Flock processes
Attend a fly day, in-person virtual demo, and virtual demo
Completed training and onboarding requirements


The First 60 Days


Signed off to be customer facing doing demos, Flock forums, etc
Supporting DFR deals and adding value to the sales team
Adding value to the product team, giving feedback on competitive landscape, where we need to improve
Help to drive the Flock sales team in understanding DFR and our strategy, helping them close deals
You’ve met the entire team listed above
Start contributing to the DFR goals / logos won


90 Days & Beyond


OKR’s have been established for Q2 2025
Mastered the pitch and objection handling
Driving pipeline & helping sales win product decision with their customers
Increase win rate for DFR 30%


The Interview Process

We want our interview process to be a true reflection of our culture: transparent and collaborative. Throughout the interview process, your recruiter will guide you through the next steps and ensure you feel prepared every step of the way.


Our First Chat: During this first conversation, you’ll meet with a recruiter to chat through your background, what you could bring to Flock, what you are looking for in your next role, and who we are.
The Hiring Manager Interview: You will meet with your potential future boss to really dive into the role, the team, expectations, and what success means at Flock. This is your chance to really nerd out with someone in your field.
The Technical Assessment: Our technical assessments seek to test the hard skills required to do the job. Engineers may find themselves in coding interviews or architecture discussions, sales roles may present mock discovery calls, and leadership roles may craft 90 day plans. Your recruiter will inform you of which assessment you will be assigned and ensure you are fully prepared for your big day.
The Panel: Learn more about the team, responsibilities, and workflows. You should be prepared to speak about past projects, how you collaborate and communicate with others, and how you live our values. Depending on the team and role you are interviewing for, you may meet with several teammates as well as cross-functional partners.
The Executive Review: A chance to meet an executive and view Flock from a different lens. Be prepared to ask well-thought-out questions about the company, culture, and more.


Compensation & Equity

This role offers a total compensation package that includes:


Base Salary: $150,000 annually
Commission: Target commission of $100,000 annually, based on performance
Equity: Stock options in Flock Safety


The final base salary will be determined based on your relevant experience, education, and market benchmarks. Your recruiter will provide a detailed overview of the compensation and equity package during your initial conversation.

The Perks

🌴Flexible PTO: We seriously mean it, plus 11 company holidays.

⚕️Fully-paid health benefits plan for employees: including Medical, Dental, and Vision and an HSA match.

👪Family Leave: All employees receive 12 weeks of 100% paid parental leave. Birthing parents are eligible for an additional 6-8 weeks of physical recovery time.

🍼Fertility & Family Benefits: We have partnered with Maven, a complete digital health benefit for starting and raising a family. Flock will provide a $50,000-lifetime maximum benefit related to eligible adoption, surrogacy, or fertility expenses.

🧠Spring Health: Spring Health offers a variety of mental health benefits, including therapy, coaching, medication management, and digital tools, all tailored to each individual's needs.

💖Caregiver Support: We have partnered with Cariloop to provide our employees with caregiver support

💸Carta Tax Advisor: Employees receive 1:1 sessions with Equity Tax Advisors who can address individual grants, model tax scenarios, and answer general questions.

💚ERGs: We want all employees to thrive and feel like they belong at Flock. We offer four ERGs today - Women of Flock, Flock Proud, LEOs and Melanin Motion. If you are interested in talking to a representative from one of these, please let your recruiter know.

💻WFH Stipend: $150 per month to cover the costs of working from home.

📚Productivity Stipend: $250 per year to use on Audible, Calm, Masterclass, Duolingo, Grammarly and so much more.

🏠Home Office Stipend: A one-time $750 to help you create your dream office.

If an offer is extended and accepted, this position requires the ability to obtain and maintain Criminal Justice Information Services (CJIS) certification as a condition of employment. Applicants must meet all FBI CJIS Security Policy requirements, including a fingerprint-based background check.

Flock is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diverse backgrounds and thoughts and welcome everyone to apply for employment with us. We are committed to fostering an environment that is inclusive, transparent, and collaborative. Mutual respect is central to how Flock operates, and we believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, experiences, and skills. We embrace our differences and know that we are stronger working together.

If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please email us at recruiting@flocksafety.com. This information will be treated as confidential and used only to determine an appropriate accommodation for the interview process.

At Flock Safety, we compensate our employees fairly for their work. Base salary is determined by job-related experience, education/training, as well as market indicators. The range above is representative of base salary only and does not include equity, sales bonus plans (when applicable) and benefits. This range may be modified in the future. This job posting may span more than one career level. 

Flock Safety is aware of fraudulent individuals and agencies falsely claiming to represent our company. All legitimate communication from Flock Safety will come from an email address ending in @flocksafety.com. We do not make job offers through messaging apps, social platforms, or unauthorized third parties, and we will never request payment or sensitive personal information during the hiring process. If you encounter suspicious outreach related to a Flock Safety role, please report it to recruiting@flocksafety.com

jobs.title: English Video Conversation Partner for AI Training
jobs.description_full:
About the job

About Babel Audio




Babel Audio works with some of the biggest technology companies in the world and thousands of participants globally to collect the audio data that will train the next generation of AI models.




About the Role




We’re looking for thoughtful and expressive individuals who can engage in authentic, natural, and emotionally rich video conversations with other participants. Each session will last five minutes and focus on meaningful get-to-know-you topics that capture genuine human connection and conversational flow.




In this role, you’ll be expected to bring warmth and presence to each exchange by listening actively, responding with curiosity, and expressing emotion naturally through your tone, facial reactions, and gestures. The goal is to create interactions that feel spontaneous and real, as if two people were meeting for the first time and connecting through open, honest dialogue.




This is a fully remote, freelance position that offers complete flexibility in when and how much you work. Applicants must be based in the United States.




About You




Fluent in English, with clear speech and strong conversational skills
Comfortable speaking confidently on camera and engaging with new people
Able to maintain good lighting, stable framing, and clear audio throughout recordings
Attentive, curious, and capable of expressing genuine warmth and emotion on video
Reliable, detail-oriented, and able to follow project guidelines carefully




Compensation




$30 per recorded hour
Additional performance-based incentives may be available




Responsibilities




Participate in recorded five minute video conversations with another participant
Engage naturally by listening, reacting, and responding with genuine interest
Maintain clear video and audio quality throughout each session




Technical Requirements




Camera capable of recording at 720p or higher with a minimum of 30 FPS
Laptop or desktop computer (tablets and phones not supported)
External or USB microphone preferred for clear, natural audio
Good lighting and a distraction-free background
Stable internet connection capable of supporting smooth video calls




Additional Information




Rates are determined by the number of recorded hours and apply specifically to this project within the United States. If your application is accepted for a different Babel Audio project, the compensation rate may vary.

jobs.title: Entry-Level AI Generalist (No Experience Required)
jobs.description_full:
About the job

We are seeking motivated individuals who are curious about artificial intelligence and excited to learn how advanced AI models are built, evaluated, and improved. This project is designed for people who are early in their academic journey, exploring potential specialties, or simply eager to gain hands-on exposure to real-world AI development projects. No prior experience is required—what matters most is your willingness to learn, ask questions, and engage thoughtfully with new concepts.

In this project, you will interact directly with AI systems, review model outputs, follow clear task guidelines, and contribute to the creation of high-quality training data. You may work on activities such as evaluating responses, identifying inconsistencies, providing feedback, or helping structure information so that models learn more effectively. All tasks are well-defined, and you will receive guidance and examples to help you succeed as you build confidence over time.

You should be comfortable working independently, paying close attention to detail, and communicating clearly about what you observe. A thoughtful, reflective approach—explaining your reasoning and noting uncertainties—is highly valued, as it helps improve both the models and the training process. This project is well-suited for individuals who enjoy problem-solving, learning through practice, and contributing to innovative technology in a structured environment.

This project offers a unique opportunity to gain practical exposure to AI development without needing a technical background. You’ll develop transferable skills in critical thinking, evaluation, and structured communication while contributing to systems that are shaping the future of technology across many industries.

If you’re excited to learn, open to feedback, and interested in being part of the process that helps AI systems improve and evolve, this project provides a supportive entry point into the world of AI training and development.

We offer a pay range of $6-to- $65 per hour, with the exact rate determined after evaluating your experience, expertise, and geographic location. Final offer amounts may vary from the pay range listed above. As a contractor you’ll supply a secure computer and high‑speed internet; company‑sponsored benefits such as health insurance and PTO do not apply.

jobs.title: Safety Tutor
jobs.description_full:
About the job

About xAI

xAI’s mission is to create AI systems that can accurately understand the universe and aid humanity in its pursuit of knowledge. Our team is small, highly motivated, and focused on engineering excellence. This organization is for individuals who appreciate challenging themselves and thrive on curiosity. We operate with a flat organizational structure. All employees are expected to be hands-on and to contribute directly to the company’s mission. Leadership is given to those who show initiative and consistently deliver excellence. Work ethic and strong prioritization skills are important. All engineers are expected to have strong communication skills. They should be able to concisely and accurately share knowledge with their teammates.

 About the Role

As an AI Tutor specialized in safety, you will contribute to xAI's mission by training and refining Grok to implement strong safeguards against misuse and vulnerabilities. Your work will directly impact the creation of AI systems that minimize existential risks, enforce ethical boundaries, and promote responsible development, helping to prevent harmful applications in critical domains.

Responsibilities

You will use proprietary software to provide labels, annotations, and inputs on projects involving safety protocols, risk scenarios, and policy compliance. You must support the delivery of high-quality curated data that reinforces AI resilience and ethical alignment. In this effort, you will collaborate with technical staff to develop tasks that improve AI's ability to detect and resist unsafe behaviors. You’ll also work with technical staff to improve annotation tools for efficient workflows.

Required Qualifications
Expertise in identifying and testing AI model vulnerabilities, including jailbreaking methods, to enhance safety mechanisms.
Deep commitment to addressing existential risks, such as those related to radiological or biological weapons.
Ability to interpret and apply AI safety policies effectively.
Proficiency in analyzing complex scenarios, with strong skills in ethical reasoning and risk assessment.
Strong ability to utilize resources, guidelines, and frameworks for accurate safety-focused annotations.
Strong communication, interpersonal, analytical, and ethical decision-making skills.
Commitment to developing AI that prioritizes safety and risk mitigation.
Preferred Qualifications
Experience in AI red-teaming, alignment research, or roles involving safety evaluations and policy development.
Location & Other Expectations
This position is based in Palo Alto, CA, or fully remote.
The Palo Alto option is an in-office role requiring 5 days per week; remote positions require strong self-motivation.
If you are based in the US, please note we are unable to hire in the states of Wyoming and Illinois at this time.
We are unable to provide visa sponsorship.
Team members are expected to work from 9:00am - 5:30pm PST for the first two weeks of training and 9:00am - 5:30pm in their own timezone thereafter.
For those who will be working from a personal device, please note your computer must be a Chromebook, Mac with MacOS 11.0 or later, or Windows 10 or later.
You must own and have reliable access to a smartphone.
Compensation

$45/hour - $100/hour

The posted pay range is intended for U.S.-based candidates and depends on factors including relevant experience, skills, education, geographic location, and qualifications. For international candidates, our recruiting team can provide an estimated pay range for your location.

Benefits:

Hourly pay is just one part of our total rewards package at xAI. Specific benefits vary by country, depending on your country of residence you may have access to medical benefits. We do not offer benefits for part-time roles.

 

xAI is an equal opportunity employer.
